data.title: 3 Outperform Stocks Flying Under the Radar in 2026
data.description:
As investors look ahead to 2026, market leadership may come from unexpected places. While Wall Street analysts often focus on consensus favorites, some of the most compelling opportunities emerge where fundamentals tell a different story than prevailing sentiment. Archer Daniels Midland (ADM), UniFirst (UNF), and Kennametal (KMT) all carry Outperform ratings and high TipRanks Smart Scores, indicating strong financial and operational foundations. Yet, despite these strengths, Wall Street analysts broadly classify them as Sell or Moderate Sell.

This disconnect between quantitative fundamentals and analyst sentiment creates a rare setup: high-quality businesses trading under a cloud of skepticism. Each of these companies is quietly executing, improving margins, strengthening cash flows, and positioning itself for durable long-term growth. For patient investors, ADM, UNF, and KMT may represent overlooked outperformers heading into 2026
data.author: George Tchetvertakov

data.title: Gold and Silver Outshine Crypto in a Record-Setting Year
data.description:
Gold (GLD) and silver (SLV) investors are having a strong year, especially when compared with crypto holders. In fact, gold futures moved above $4,550 on Friday and remained near record highs after setting more than 50 new records this year. At the same time, silver surged past $75 per ounce, thereby pushing its year-to-date gains to roughly 150%. Interestingly, Silver's rally has been fueled by worries about physical shortages due to strong industrial demand. Other metals have also joined the surge, with platinum (PPLT) and copper (COP) both hitting record highs this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, cryptocurrencies have moved in the opposite direction. Notably, Bitcoin is on track to end the year in negative territory and is trying to avoid a third straight losing month. The world's largest cryptocurrency has also diverged from stocks for the first time since 2014, despite friendlier regulation and growing interest from Wall Street. More specifically, after long-term holders sold and forced liquidations kicked in, Bitcoin fell roughly 30% from its October high of nearly $126,000 to just over $87,000.

As a result, critics such as Louis Navellier and gold bull Peter Schiff argue that investors may be better off buying gold due to central bank buying, lower volatility, and stronger liquidity. Nevertheless, some strategists believe that crypto may rebound. For instance, Fundstrat's head of digital assets, Sean Farrell, said that he expects a bounce in January if December closes in the red, since similar patterns have historically led to recoveries.

Is Bitcoin a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a negative outlook for Bitcoin. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that two indicators are Bullish, compared to six Neutral and 14 Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Stall on New China Sanctions ahead of Trump-Zelenskyy Meeting
data.description:
It was a quiet day for both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) on the second day of the "Santa Rally" as both ETFs closed near breakeven.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, followers of seasonality were let down, as December 26 has historically been the second-strongest day of the year for the S&P 500 (SPX) with an average gain of 0.49%, according to data compiled by Carson Investment Research.

The day kicked off with China announcing new sanctions against 20 U.S. defense firms and 10 defense executives for selling arms to Taiwan, including Boeing's (BA) St. Louis defense branch, L3Harris (LHX) Maritime Services, Northrop Grumman (NOC), and Anduril's Palmer Luckey. The action comes after the U.S. agreed to sell $11.1 billion of weapons to Taiwan last week.

"The Taiwan issue is the core of China's core interests and the first red line that cannot be crossed in China-U.S. relations," said a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson.

Meanwhile, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will meet with President Trump over the weekend to discuss a 20-point peace plan to end the Russia-Ukraine war. Zelenskyy added that the plan is nearly ready, although the two sides still need to negotiate territorial concessions and control of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant.

"The 20-point plan we have been working on is 90% ready," Zelenskyy said. "Our task is to make sure everything is 100% ready. This is not easy, and no one says that 100% will happen immediately." The plan's terms include a reaffirmation of Ukraine's sovereignty, the return of prisoners of war, and Ukraine becoming a member of the European Union (EU).

The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are set to post double-digit returns this year, with tech billionaires emerging as the biggest winners. The net worths of the top U.S. tech CEOs and founders have increased by over $550 billion to $2.5 trillion in 2025, according to Bloomberg.

Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk's fortune surged to $746 billion this year, ranking him as the richest person in the world. Last week, the Delaware Supreme Court reinstated Musk's pay package, giving the billionaire a $139 billion boost. Google (GOOGL) cofounder Larry Page is the second-richest person in the world with a net worth of $257 billion, followed by Oracle (ORCL) cofounder Larry Ellison at $249 billion.

The S&P 500 closed with a 0.03% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 fell by 0.05%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why IBM Is Well-Positioned to Become a Quantum Computing Leader
data.description:
IBM (IBM) is well-positioned to emerge as a leader in quantum computing as the technology moves closer to real-world usefulness, according to Forbes. The tech company recently outlined its progress at the IBM Quantum Developers Conference, where it shared a clear roadmap to achieve "quantum advantage" as early as next year. For context, that would be the point where quantum computers outperform traditional machines for specific tasks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

IBM's approach stands out because it focuses not just on adding more qubits, but also on improving hardware, software, error correction, and fabrication all at once. Interestingly, a key part of this strategy is the firm's steady rollout of more powerful quantum processors. Indeed, IBM recently introduced its Nighthawk chip, which features 120 qubits and faster speeds compared to earlier designs. In addition, IBM plans to continue scaling its systems through 2028 in order to eventually link many chips together to perform more complex operations.

At the same time, IBM is upgrading its Qiskit software platform to make it easier for developers to build and test quantum applications. IBM's advantage also comes from combining its deep research experience with tools that are already used by a broad developer community. Therefore, if the company can execute on its roadmap, it could play a key role in the early commercialization of quantum computing.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 10 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $303.71 per share implies that shares are trading near fair value.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Alphabet Stock (GOOGL) Has Outperformed Magnificent 7 Peers. Does Wall Street See More Upside?
data.description:

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Uses Big Tech Playbook to Cut Antitrust Risk in $20B Groq Deal
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) $20 billion deal with AI chip startup Groq has drawn attention not because of what was announced, but because of how little was said. Despite the size of the transaction, Nvidia has not issued a press release or filed regulatory paperwork. Instead, it only confirmed the contents of a short Groq blog post. This led five-star Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon to claim on CNBC's Squawk on the Street that Nvidia has become so large that it can do a $20 billion deal on Christmas Eve, "and nobody bats an eye."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, Rasgon made it clear that this deal is not a traditional acquisition, even though it would have been Nvidia's largest ever if structured that way. Instead, Nvidia is following a playbook that has been used by other tech giants like Meta (META), Google (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN), which have spent billions of dollars to secure AI talent and license technology without buying companies outright.

Indeed, under the agreement, Groq founder and CEO Jonathan Ross, president Sunny Madra, and other senior leaders will join Nvidia to help scale the licensed technology. At the same time, Groq will continue operating as an independent company under finance chief Simon Edwards. Rasgon noted that this approach helps companies move quickly while reducing antitrust risk, especially by framing the deal as a non-exclusive license. As a result, he reiterated Bernstein's Buy rating on Nvidia and a $275 price target.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $263.58 per share implies 37.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Dollar Set for Worst Year Since 2017 with 'Comfortably Negative' Outlook
data.description:
The U.S. dollar is on track to finish 2025 with its worst performance in eight years and further rate cuts could spur even more weakness. The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the dollar against a basket of other currencies, has shed 9.6% year-to-date.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rate cuts are generally bad news for the dollar, as they reduce the yield on U.S. assets and make them less attractive to investors. Lower rates can also lead to less demand for the greenback. Additionally, the start of the U.S. rate-cutting cycle comes as many other central banks around the globe wrap up their respective easing cycles.

Options Imply Further Pain for the U.S. Dollar

More downside could be on the way, as options pricing data shows that traders are the most bearish on the dollar in three months.

"The dollar outlook remains comfortably negative," wrote Swissquote Senior Analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya. "Bullish calls on the dollar are rare." Ozkardeskaya added that the dollar has been hampered by U.S. debt concerns and geopolitical tensions. At the same time, she noted that the dollar could turn higher if the Fed takes on a hawkish approach to rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) a Top Stock Split Candidate in 2026
data.description:
Rumors persist that U.S. pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly (LLY) will split its stock in the New Year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

LLY stock has been red hot as the company takes a commanding lead in the global market for weight-loss drugs. So far in 2025, Eli Lilly's share price has risen 40% to trade at $1,071.64 per share. Analysts say the current share price is a barrier to entry for many retail investors and makes a stock split likely in 2026.

While stock splits don't change the fundamentals of a company, they do lower the share price and make it more affordable for individual investors. For this reason, stocks often receive a boost after they undertake a split. Companies that have split their stocks in recent years include Nvidia (NVDA) and Apple (AAPL).

Eli Lilly's Stock Split History

For its part, Eli Lilly hasn't split its stock in nearly 30 years. The last time Eli Lilly executed a stock split was in October 1997, when it underwent a 2-for-1 division. Each stockholder received one additional share for every one they held while the trading price was cut in half.

Eli Lilly also undertook 2-for-1 stock splits in 1995, 1989 and 1986. The company has been publicly traded since 1952. Despite its rapid growth in recent years, fueled by its popular weight-loss drugs, Eli Lilly has not announced a stock split even as its shares trade above $1,000 each.

A growing number of analysts say that is likely to change in 2026 as the company launches a weight-loss pill that is expected to boost sales by billions of dollars and lead to further gains in LLY stock.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,135.32 implies 5.94% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Xpeng Stock (XPEV) Surges as It Seizes Key Opportunity in the Middle East
data.description:
Shares of Xpeng (XPEV) are surging today after the Chinese EV maker announced plans to expand into the Middle East and Africa. More precisely, the company said that it will enter the Qatari market through a partnership with a subsidiary of Almana Group, which will serve as its exclusive distributor in the country. At the same time, Xpeng is teaming up with Axess Limited to bring its vehicles to Mauritius in East Africa. Xpeng will begin by launching its G6 and G9 SUV models in Qatar, with the P7+ sedan expected to follow at a later date.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, the firm stated that Qatar will act as an important gateway for connecting with global customers while demonstrating its smart mobility technology. It is also worth noting that the company already operates an experience center in Abu Dhabi, a showroom and service center in Egypt, and an accessories warehouse in Dubai. Unsurprisingly, Xpeng is hoping to close the gap with BYD (BYDDF), which is currently the largest Chinese EV brand in the Middle East and North Africa.

What's impressive is that Chinese EVs are expected to capture 34% of the Middle East and Africa market by 2030, up from 10% in 2024, according to AlixPartners. Even more important is that nearly three-quarters of EV buyers in the Middle East say they trust Chinese-made vehicles, which is more than twice the share who trust U.S.-made EVs. Therefore, this makes the region a key opportunity for Xpeng.

Is XPEV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XPEV stock based on four Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average XPEV price target of $29.67 per share implies 41.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
